findings.description: Nested <main> landmark elements - ensure the document has at most one main landmark at top level
findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 39. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: Ensure the complementary landmark or aside is at top level
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  The null landmark is contained in another landmark. [<aside>]

findings.description: The welcome slide has a two-panel layout (left panel with audio troubleshooting, right panel with welcome text) that is collapsed into a single column in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows a left blue panel with audio troubleshooting info and a right cork-board panel with 'Welcome to the CHPP Steering Committee Meeting'. HTML renders these as sequential single-column content.

findings.description: The decorative cork-board background texture on the right panel of the welcome slide is missing in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 right panel has a distinctive cork-board texture background. HTML shows plain white background.

findings.description: The Coastal Habitat Protection Plan Timeline slide has a two-panel layout (table on left, stacked wildlife images on right) that is collapsed to single column in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 shows the timeline table on the left with a vertical strip of nature/wildlife photos on the right side. HTML renders the images separately above the table in a single column.

findings.description: The wildlife/nature images from the timeline slide are placed above the table rather than alongside it, changing their contextual relationship.
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 has images stacked vertically on the right side of the table. HTML places them above the table in a different arrangement.

findings.description: Nested <main> element inside the first <section> creates invalid HTML structure and the first section uses an <aside> for the audio troubleshooting content alongside a nested <main>, which is semantically incorrect (two <main> elements).
findings.evidence: The HTML contains <main> as the outer wrapper, then inside the first <section> there is another <main> element wrapping the welcome content. This is invalid HTML structure with duplicate <main> landmarks.

findings.description: The page 3 heading 'Coastal Habitat Protection Plan Timeline' is rendered as h2 but in the PDF, 'Coastal Habitat Protection Plan' is the main title and 'Timeline' is a subtitle. This is a minor formatting difference but acceptable.
findings.evidence:
PDF shows 'Coastal Habitat Protection Plan' as large title with 'Timeline' as subtitle below. HTML combines them as '

Coastal Habitat Protection Plan Timeline

'.

findings.description: Alt text for images on page 3 is partially fabricated or inaccurate. The PDF page 3 shows a vertical strip of wildlife/habitat photos on the right side, but the HTML splits them into three separate figure elements with specific alt texts that may not precisely match each individual image.
findings.evidence: HTML has three separate figures with alt texts: 'Composite image showing various coastal wildlife and habitats', 'Egret wading in a marsh', 'Scallop on a seagrass bed'. The PDF shows a continuous strip of approximately 5 stacked images including an egret, fish, oysters, a horseshoe crab/scallop, and what appears to be a sea turtle or fish on coral.

findings.description: The page number '3' shown on page 3 of the PDF is not in the HTML, but this is acceptable page number removal.
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 shows '3' at bottom. HTML omits it.
